card.hook: A browser extension can watch wallet-facing flows, but MV3 and content-script permission limits mean it cannot assume universal blocking or visibility. That pushes the core pre-signing verdict onto contract readability, proxy resolution, EIP-712 interpretation, and simulation rather than page-level trust signals alone.
card.why_it_matters: If you are learning to build secure browser-extension tooling around smart contracts, this gives you a concrete architecture boundary: detection in the page, enforcement at signing, and explicit handling of privacy tradeoffs in simulation.
card.action: Write a 3-step verdict sketch for a single signing request that lists: (1) how you would check verified ABI and proxy-to-implementation resolution, (2) how you would classify an EIP-712 signature or transaction simulation result, and (3) the exact condition that triggers block vs warn.
